youtube_items.title: [YT] Why are so Many People going Hungry? | World Hunger | FAO Explainer | United Nations
youtube_items.summary: [DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] If you're watching this, chances are that you are in the fortunate position to have access to the food you want, when you need it.

analysis.problem: Conflict, economic shocks, climate extremes, and forced displacement are driving food insecurity and malnutrition globally
analysis.root_cause: The combination of human-induced climate change, socio-economic inequality, and geopolitical tensions leading to conflicts and displacement
leverage_points: International cooperation and diplomacy to resolve conflicts and address climate change
Investment in climate-resilient agriculture and social protection programs

proposed_actions.action: Implement climate-resilient agricultural practices and support small-scale farmers
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of people facing food insecurity by 20% within the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Establish emergency food assistance programs for affected populations
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Provide food assistance to at least 80% of the affected population within the next 2 years

evidence: The Global Report on Food Crises 2025 shows that conflict, economic shocks, climate extremes, and forced displacement continued to drive food insecurity and malnutrition around the world
The 2025 SOFI report from FAO, IFAD, UNICEF, WFP, and WHO examines how rising food prices are shaping access to healthy diets and driving food insecurity and malnutrition
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Civilization (socio-economic inequality, conflict) and Planet (climate change, environmental degradation)
analysis.summary: The global food system is facing a critical crisis driven by conflict, climate change, and socio-economic inequality, threatening the dignity and well-being of millions of people. Urgent actions are needed to address the root causes and mitigate the effects of food insecurity and malnutrition.

raw_items.title: Global Report on Food Crises (GRFC) 2025 - World Food Programme
raw_items.summary: Jul 11, 2025 · The Global Report on Food Crises (GRFC) 2025 shows conflict, economic shocks, climate extremes and forced displacement continued to drive food insecurity and malnutrition around the world, with a catastrophic impact on many already fragile regions.

raw_items.title: The State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World 2025
raw_items.summary: Aug 8, 2025 · The 2025 SOFI report from FAO, IFAD, UNICEF, WFP and WHO examines how rising food prices are shaping access to healthy diets and driving food insecurity and malnutrition.

raw_items.title: Assessing the vulnerability of food supply chains to climate change-induced disruptions - PubMed
raw_items.summary: April 10, 2024 - The vulnerability assessment methods as well as the adaptation and resilience measures that have been employed to alleviate the impact of climate change in the food supply chain were discussed. The results revealed a growing number of publications providing evidence of the weakening of the food supply chain due to climate change and extreme weather events.
